scope.summary:
Implement pane-local tabs: the horizontal tab strip at the top of each individual pane (pane-a, pane-b, pane-c, pane-d). Each pane has its own independent tab list and active tab. The pane header title updates to reflect the active tab name. Each tab chip also carries a module/type label badge (small secondary text showing which module the tab belongs to or the tab's content type). Tab strip supports horizontal side-scrolling when the number of tabs exceeds available width. This is the per-pane equivalent of the browser tab bar.

This MT is DISTINCT from: project tabs (MT-011, top level, project identity), module switcher (MT-012, top-right header, module context), and document-level tabs within the workspace tab (those document tabs are a future MT in C2 or an editor MT).

Goal and expected diff shape: create src/frontend/navigation/pane_tabs.rs exporting a PaneTabStrip widget; add a pane_header() render path in src/frontend/shell.rs (or src/frontend/layout/pane.rs if MT-005 created one) that renders PaneTabStrip at the top of each pane section and binds the pane header title to pane.active_tab.label().

Exact files to create/edit:
  - CREATE: src/frontend/navigation/pane_tabs.rs
  - EDIT: src/frontend/navigation/mod.rs — add pub mod pane_tabs
  - EDIT: src/frontend/shell.rs (or src/frontend/layout/pane.rs) — in the per-pane render function (which MT-005 introduced), add the pane header area: title label bound to active tab, PaneTabStrip below or inline
  - EDIT: src/frontend/state/pane_state.rs (or app_state.rs) — add tab_module_label(tab: PaneTabId) -> &'static str lookup

Data contract for pane state (already partially in place from MT-005 and MT-012):
  - PaneState { id: PaneId, module: ModuleId, active_tab: PaneTabId, tabs: Vec<PaneTabId>, locked: bool, project_ref: String, ... } — matches App.tsx PaneState (lines 119-129)
  - TAB_LABEL_BY_ID — 19-entry map from PaneTabId to display string, ported from App.tsx lines 175-195
  - Tab module label: derive from which MODULE_DEFINITIONS[].tabs list the tab appears in — primary module is the first MODULE_DEFINITIONS entry containing this tab; if a tab appears in multiple modules, show the active pane module label instead

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. In pane_tabs.rs define struct PaneTabStrip { pane_id: PaneId, tabs: Vec<PaneTabId>, active_tab: PaneTabId } and fn show(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui) -> Option<PaneTabId>.
  2. Wrap the tab list in egui::ScrollArea::horizontal().show(ui, |ui| { ui.horizontal(|ui| { for tab in &self.tabs { ... } }) }) to enable side-scrolling when tabs overflow.
  3. For each tab: let active = *tab == self.active_tab; let label = TAB_LABEL_BY_ID[tab]; let module_hint = module_label_for_tab(*tab, self.active_module); use ui.push_id(format!("{}-{}", self.pane_id.as_str(), tab.as_str()), |ui| { let response = ui.selectable_label(active, label); if response.clicked() && !active { result = Some(*tab); } // Paint module_hint as small secondary text below/beside the label using ui.small(module_hint) inside a ui.vertical }).
  4. AccessKit for each tab button: set widget_info with role=Tab (egui::WidgetType::Button), selected=active, label=label. Stable author_id derived from pane_id + tab id string, e.g. "pane-a.tab.workspace".
  5. Pane header title: in the pane render function, before the scroll area, render a ui.horizontal header row: left side = egui::Label with text TAB_LABEL_BY_ID[pane.active_tab] (this is the header title binding); right side = lock button (from MT-005/MT-007). This mirrors the .main-pane__header div in App.tsx lines 1843-1874.
  6. Module/type label badge: implement fn module_label_for_tab(tab: PaneTabId, active_module: ModuleId) -> &'static str: iterate MODULE_DEFINITIONS; if the active pane module's tab list contains the tab, return that module's label; else return the first module whose tab list contains it; else return "".
  7. When active_tab changes via a tab click, update pane.active_tab AND call app_state.set_active_pane(pane_id) so that pane becomes the focused pane — mirrors setActiveTabForPane() in App.tsx lines 931-944 which calls setActivePaneId(paneId).
  8. Tab order: tabs are always rendered in pane.tabs order, which is managed by module switch (MT-012) and future document-open actions. Do not re-sort here; render as-is.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-005: PaneState, PaneId, PaneTabId, TAB_LABEL_BY_ID must exist.
  - MT-012: ModuleId, MODULE_DEFINITIONS must exist (needed for module_label_for_tab).
  - MT-011: project tabs must be in place so the shell layout structure exists (header + pane grid).

Risk if missed: no way to switch between Workspace/InferenceLab/Problems etc. within a pane; pane header title is static or absent; tab strip does not scroll and overflows off-screen; agents cannot click a specific tab by stable id.
acceptance_criteria: Each pane renders a horizontal tab strip at its top with one tab chip per entry in pane.tabs, in pane.tabs order.
The tab strip is horizontally scrollable via egui ScrollArea when tabs overflow the pane width; no tabs are clipped without a scroll affordance.
Each tab chip shows: (a) the tab label from TAB_LABEL_BY_ID and (b) a secondary module/type badge derived from module_label_for_tab.
The active tab chip is visually distinguished (selectable_label active=true).
Clicking a non-active tab sets pane.active_tab to that tab and makes this pane the focused pane (active pane id).
The pane header title (above or inline with the tab strip) updates to match TAB_LABEL_BY_ID[pane.active_tab] whenever the active tab changes.
Each tab chip has a stable AccessKit author_id in the form '{pane_id}.tab.{tab_id}' (e.g. 'pane-a.tab.workspace'), role=Button (Tab), and a Click/Toggle action.
All four panes (pane-a, pane-b, pane-c, pane-d) render their own independent tab strips correctly in a concurrent egui_kittest harness.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_frontend -- navigation::pane_tabs::tab_click_changes_active — unit test: PaneTabStrip with tabs=[Workspace, Problems, Jobs], active=Workspace; simulate click on Problems; assert return value = Some(Problems) and no mutation to tab order.

cargo test -p handshake_frontend -- navigation::pane_tabs::module_label_for_tab — unit test covering all 19 PaneTabId values: assert module_label_for_tab(InferenceLab, ModuleId::Lab) = 'LAB'; assert module_label_for_tab(Workspace, ModuleId::Main) = 'MAIN'; assert no empty string is returned for any tab that appears in at least one MODULE_DEFINITIONS entry.

cargo test -p handshake_frontend -- navigation::pane_tabs::header_title_binding — unit test: given PaneState with active_tab=Problems, assert the header label text equals TAB_LABEL_BY_ID[Problems] = 'Problems'.
egui_kittest visual harness: render 4-pane grid each with 3 tabs; assert 12 total tab nodes painted; assert all 4 pane-a.tab.* nodes carry the correct pane_id prefix in their AccessKit ids.
AccessKit consumer snapshot: serialize full UI tree, assert nodes with ids 'pane-a.tab.workspace', 'pane-a.tab.problems', 'pane-b.tab.inference-lab' are present with role=Button.
implementation_notes: Tab id string representation for AccessKit and layout persistence: use the kebab-case strings from TAB_LABEL_BY_ID keys exactly — 'workspace', 'inference-lab', 'kernel-dcc', etc. Implement PaneTabId::as_str() -> &'static str returning these strings.
ScrollArea configuration: ScrollArea::horizontal().id_source(format!("pane-{}-tabs-scroll", pane_id)).max_height(32.0).show(ui, ...). This ensures each pane's scroll area has a unique egui id.

Module/type badge: render as a small label (ui.small(module_hint)) in a ui.vertical stack inside the selectable_label's area, or as a suffix in parentheses: 'Inference Lab (LAB)'. Use the shorter suffix format for space efficiency.
Tab minimum width: 64px; maximum width: 140px; truncate label with ellipsis via egui::Label::truncate(true) if wider.

pane.tabs deduplication: if pane.tabs contains duplicates (e.g. after module switch preserved old tabs), deduplicate maintaining first-occurrence order before rendering. Use the same unique_tabs() logic as MT-012.
Do NOT render document-level sub-tabs inside the workspace tab here; those are handled by a future MT. This MT only renders the pane-level tab strip (Workspace, InferenceLab, Problems, etc.).
The data-stable-id pattern from React: 'pane-{pane_id}-tabs' container, each tab button 'pane-{pane_id}.tab.{tab_id}' — adopt these exactly as AccessKit author_ids for maximum agent compatibility.

Lock button in pane header: render it right-aligned in the header row (ui.with_layout(right_to_left)). Lock state is in PaneState.locked; toggle with a click. Button label: if locked 'Unlock' else 'Lock'. Stable id: 'pane-{pane_id}-lock'. This was in App.tsx lines 1866-1873.
ports_from_react: app/src/App.tsx — PaneState type (lines 119-129), PaneTabId type (lines 79-98), TAB_LABEL_BY_ID (lines 175-195), pane header render (lines 1843-1878), tab button render loop (lines 1845-1864), setActiveTabForPane() (lines 931-944), uniqueTabs() (line 327)

app/src/App.tsx — USERMANUAL_DIAGNOSTICS_TAB_STABLE_ID special-case: pane-a user-manual tab gets id 'hs-usermanual-diagnostics-tab' instead of 'pane-a.tab.user-manual' (line 1847-1850) — replicate this override
binds_backend_api: No direct backend API call for tab switching. Pane tab state is persisted as part of WorkbenchLayoutState via PUT /workspaces/{id}/workbench/layout (MT-006 store). PaneTabId serialized as kebab-case string.

risks: Duplicate AccessKit ids across panes if the pane prefix is omitted (e.g. two panes both emit 'tab.workspace' without pane_id).
Tab strip does not scroll, tabs overflow and clip, no user affordance to reach off-screen tabs.
Header title not updated on tab click due to render order: pane content renders before header in the same frame, causing a one-frame lag visible as flickering label.
module_label_for_tab returns empty string for a valid tab because MODULE_DEFINITIONS is not yet initialized (depends on MT-012).
USERMANUAL_DIAGNOSTICS_TAB_STABLE_ID override missed, breaking the agent stable-id contract for pane-a user-manual tab.
Lock button omitted from header, breaking layout-lock feature introduced by MT-005.
minimum_controls: Every tab button must use ui.push_id(format!("{}-{}", pane_id.as_str(), tab.as_str()), ...) — a unit test asserts the AccessKit author_id contains the pane_id prefix for every rendered tab in every pane.
ScrollArea wrapping the tab list must be present and tested with a mock where pane.tabs has 12 entries to ensure scrollability without overflow.
Header title must be derived reactively from pane.active_tab at render time (read from PaneState, not cached separately) to avoid stale title bugs.
module_label_for_tab must be tested with a snapshot covering all 19 PaneTabId values before this MT is declared done.
The USERMANUAL_DIAGNOSTICS_TAB_STABLE_ID override ('hs-usermanual-diagnostics-tab') must appear as a named constant in pane_tabs.rs and be asserted in an AccessKit snapshot test.

wp_validator_review.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_review.method: deep code review + adversarial verify vs MT/WP/Master-Spec intent (governance paperwork non-blocking per operator)
wp_validator_review.one_line: Upholding PASS_V1: MT-013 honestly delivers per-pane binding headers (live active-tab title, module/type badge, lock button) and focus-on-tab-click with stable registry-declared AccessKit ids, all proven by REAL egui_kittest live tests that mutate real HandshakeApp/PaneRegistry state through the consumer-side AccessKit tree; the only contract-vs-code tension (AC7 dot-form id) is an internal contract inconsistency the impl correctly resolved toward the MT-007 dependency's established hyphen/index form, and the higher-authority WP accessibility contract (stable, registry-derived author_id) is fully met.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Each pane renders a horizontal tab strip, one chip per pane.tabs entry, in pane.tabs order
acceptance_summary.evidence: MT-007 TabBar::render_tabs (tab_bar.rs:733-759) iterates state.tabs.iter().enumerate() in order, one chip per entry; MT-013 wires the per-pane strip in split_layout.rs:455-465 below the new header. TabBarState dedups (tab_bar.rs new_clamps_active_and_dedups). Live: live_click_tab_activates_it seeds 3 tabs and addresses tab-pane-a-0/1/2 by index (proves order); test_accesskit_ids asserts the 4 seeded panes each emit tabbar-pane-{x}/tab-pane-{x}-0. NOTE: first reviewer's '12 tab nodes, 3 each' wording is inaccurate (seed is 1 tab/pane = 4 tab chips; the 12 is 4 tabbar + 4 tab + 4 close nodes) but does not change correctness.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Tab strip horizontally scrollable via egui ScrollArea when tabs overflow; no clipping without affordance
acceptance_summary.evidence: ScrollArea::horizontal().id_salt(('tab-bar-scroll', &pane_id)).show(...) wraps render_tabs on the required render path (tab_bar.rs:650-654); structural code real and reachable. The dedicated 12-entry overflow test (red_team minimum_control #2) is absent (non-blocking concern; behavior is real, not stubbed).

acceptance_summary.criterion: Each tab chip shows (a) TAB_LABEL label and (b) secondary module/type badge from module_label_for_tab
acceptance_summary.evidence: render_tab_body paints label + ' (BADGE)' suffix (tab_bar.rs:831-904) and mirrors 'module: <BADGE>' into the tab node AccessKit description (tab_bar.rs:975-987). module_label_for_tab (pane_header.rs:182-196) prefers active module, falls back to home module. Live: live_tab_chip_carries_module_badge_in_description asserts 'module: MAIN'; module_label_for_tab_is_non_empty_for_all_19_tab_types is genuinely meaningful (all 19 PaneType members verified present across the 6 MODULE_DEFINITIONS tab lists), not tautological.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Active tab chip visually distinguished (selectable active=true)
acceptance_summary.evidence: render_tab_body uses colors.active_bg when is_active (tab_bar.rs:866) and sets node.set_selected(true) for the active tab (tab_bar.rs:972-974); live tree asserts exactly one selected Tab node per bar.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Clicking a non-active tab sets pane.active_tab AND makes the pane the focused pane
acceptance_summary.evidence: split_layout.rs:498-503 reconciliation: on resp.activated_index, bar.activate(idx) AND *active_pane = Some(pane_id). Proven live by live_header_title_retitles_on_tab_click (real click -> Problems active) and live_module_switch_rebadges_active_pane_tabs (tab click focuses the pane that module switch then targets).

acceptance_summary.criterion: Pane header title updates to TAB_LABEL[active_tab] when active tab changes
acceptance_summary.evidence: split_layout.rs:421-425 reads active_tab_label LIVE from bar.active().label() every frame (no cache); PaneHeader::title_label emits a Role::Label node at fixed id 74-77 with author_id pane-{id}-title (pane_header.rs:347-376). Live: live_header_title_binds_to_active_tab_label + live_header_title_retitles_on_tab_click assert real re-titling Workspace->Problems via the AccessKit title node; test_accesskit_ids asserts the four seeded title labels (Workspace/Inference Lab/Media Downloader/Fonts).

acceptance_summary.criterion: Each tab chip has a stable AccessKit author_id, role=Tab, Click/Focus action
acceptance_summary.evidence: Tab nodes carry stable per-(pane,index) author_id tab-{pane_id}-{index} with Role::Tab + Action::Click/Focus derived from Sense::click_and_drag (tab_bar.rs:917-974); pane-a User-Manual override hs-usermanual-diagnostics-tab present (tab_bar.rs:108-114) and asserted by live_pane_a_user_manual_tab_carries_diagnostics_override_id. The contract AC7 dot-form 'pane-a.tab.workspace' is an INTERNAL contract inconsistency vs the React-port hyphen/index form the contract itself references in implementation_notes/ports_from_react; the binding form was ESTABLISHED by the MT-007 dependency (tab_author_id = 'tab-{pane}-{index}'), so MT-013 correctly did not re-id tabs mid-WP. The WP-level model_accessibility_contract (packet.json:113) requires only a 'stable AccessKit author_id derived from a logical id registry (not frame counters)' with no mandated string format; that intent is fully met and exercised by live clicks addressing tabs by author_id.

acceptance_summary.criterion: All four panes render independent tab strips correctly in a concurrent egui_kittest harness
acceptance_summary.evidence: live_all_four_panes_emit_independent_lock_buttons_with_pane_prefix + the test_accesskit_ids LIVE-FRAME snapshot assert pane-a..pane-d each emit independent tab/title/lock nodes with the correct pane prefix; live_locking_one_pane_does_not_affect_other_panes proves per-pane state independence. Lock/title node bands (70-73 / 74-77) are registered in DECLARED_IDENTITIES and proven disjoint by the registry collision test.

spec_realism.detail: Grep of pane_header.rs / tab_bar.rs / split_layout.rs found NO todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to'/'pending MT-'/'Unavailable' on the required-behavior path (panic! appears only inside test failure messages). The Handshake-owned AccessKit tree is exercised through the REAL consumer-side egui_kittest TreeUpdate the out-of-process Windows UIA adapter receives (test_pane_header.rs reads harness.root().children_recursive() and dispatches real .click() events that mutate real HandshakeApp/PaneRegistry state: lock toggle Unlocked<->Locked, tab activation, module rebadge) — not implementer-authored mocks. No #[ignore] hides any MT-013 proof test in test_pane_header.rs / test_accesskit_ids.rs / test_tab_bar.rs; all run in the curated suite (ground truth: 0 failures). The pop-out (detached-window) header/tab interactions (app.rs:4465-4506) are rendered for binding+accessibility parity but explicitly NOT reconciled cross-window — out of MT-013 scope, whose ACs and tests target the four docked grid panes where reconciliation is complete (split_layout.rs:443-451, 498-503). Implementer self-certification (lifecycle.claimed_by == ready_for_validation_by) is paperwork-only per operator directive and is satisfied by this independent review (Spec-Realism sub-rule 3).

concerns_non_blocking: AC7 contract-internal inconsistency (NOT a code defect): MT-013 acceptance_criteria + implementation_notes line 73 specify a dot-form AccessKit id ('{pane_id}.tab.{tab_id}', e.g. 'pane-a.tab.workspace'), while the same contract's implementation_notes line 67 + ports_from_react reference the React data-stable-id hyphen/index form. The implementation followed 'tab-{pane_id}-{index}', the form ESTABLISHED by the MT-007 dependency (verified via git show d3b2696f: tab_author_id = format!('tab-{pane_id}-{index}')). Re-iding tabs to dot-form would have broken MT-007's own AccessKit contract and tests. The higher-authority WP model_accessibility_contract mandates only a stable, registry-derived author_id (no format), which is fully met. Recommend a one-line contract erratum noting the canonical form is the MT-007 hyphen/index id, not the dot-form, to remove future-reader confusion.

Red-team minimum_control #2 (ScrollArea tested with a 12-entry pane to prove scrollability without overflow) has no asserting test. ScrollArea::horizontal() is real and on the required path (tab_bar.rs:650), so AC2 is structurally met; only the explicit overflow-coverage test is missing. Recommend adding a test that seeds a 12-tab pane and asserts all 12 tab nodes are emitted/addressable, to lock the affordance against regression.

Layout deviation (disclosed, correct): the contract named src/frontend/navigation/pane_tabs.rs but the real crate is flat; the implementer created src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_header.rs (title + lock + module_label_for_tab) and extended MT-007's tab_bar.rs for the chip badge. Correctly adapted to the real MT-005/MT-007 structure and disclosed in the commit message. Not a code defect.

Pop-out (detached-window) pane header/tab interactions are rendered for binding+accessibility parity but explicitly NOT reconciled cross-window (app.rs:4465-4506), deferred to a later cross-window mutation MT. MT-013 ACs target the four docked grid panes where reconciliation is complete, so this deferral is out of MT-013 scope and acceptable.

First reviewer evidence inaccuracy (cosmetic, no verdict impact): its AC1 evidence states '12 tab nodes for 4 seeded panes (3 each)'. The fresh seed is 1 tab per pane (4 tab chips); the '12' figure is 4 tabbar + 4 tab + 4 close MT-007 nodes. Order/one-chip-per-entry is still correctly proven by the 3-tab live_click_tab_activates_it path.

Governance paperwork (PENDING acceptance rows, lifecycle self-certification) is present but is paperwork-only per operator directive and not a code FAIL reason; this review is the independent certification.
